title: Judith Butler: Life, Philosophy, Politics, Ethics: E-Special Issue Introduction
author: Loizidou

opening_move.description: Loizidou introduces an e-special issue devoted to Judith Butler by situating Butler's work across its full trajectory (from Gender Trouble through Frames of War and The Force of Non-Violence) and by presenting Butler as a philosopher whose commitment to thinking the impossible and to solidarity with the excluded has produced both cultural iconicity and sustained theoretical influence across sociology, gender studies, politics, and the arts.
anchored_in: Butler (Gender Trouble, Frames of War, The Force of Non-Violence)
opening_move.stakes_claimed: A comprehensive introduction to Butler's philosophical development, their influence on TCS, and the arc of their thought from gender performativity to ethics, biopolitics, and non-violence — providing context for the assembled collection.

theoretical_positioning.description: This is an e-special issue introduction with no argumentative position of its own. It presents and contextualizes Butler's work and the issue's contents. The paper's function is biographical, intellectual-historical, and curatorial.

epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: Intellectual biography and hermeneutic presentation of Butler's philosophical development; contextualization within TCS journal history; juxtaposition of Butler's earlier and later writings.
epistemic_mode.notes: E-special issue introduction — similar format to the Featherstone-Turner Douglas interview introduction. The paper's function is archival/curatorial rather than argumentative. No normative position is advanced; the epistemic mode is purely_interpretive_hermeneutic in the sense that the paper presents and contextualizes Butler's intellectual biography.

case_type.case_name: Judith Butler's corpus (Gender Trouble through The Force of Non-Violence)

normative_stake.domain: feminist philosophy, gender studies, intellectual biography

confidence_notes: E-special issue introduction — archival/curatorial format, no independent argument. Analogous to the Featherstone-Turner interview introduction in function. The 'purely_interpretive_hermeneutic' mode captures the absence of a normative argument: the paper presents Butler's work and provides biographical-intellectual context for the assembled collection. No normative stake of its own.
